goal: Remove the temporary cell entrance helper systems and recently generated Roblox Terrain now that the scene is moving to a total mesh setup.
changes: Removed the generated cell wall pore collision system from the builder.
Removed the CellEntryPoreGuide visual markers, beacon spheres, bubbles, beams, light, and mist.
Removed the generated Terrain water volume creation path.
Changed clearGeneratedTerrain after play testing so each build preserves user-edited Workspace.Terrain instead of deleting manually added water.
Kept the submarine-side spawn at Vector3.new(0, 58, -385.691).
Left imported mesh packages and organelle assembly intact so the total mesh workflow can define the visible cell.
validation: Deployed BuildMainCellExperience.lua into Roblox Studio through the legacy bridge.
Ran require(builder).build() and confirmed no entrance guide, no cell wall collision bounds, and no automatic Terrain deletion.
result: The scene builder no longer creates the temporary entry system or Terrain water volume, and it now preserves manually edited Studio water when Play starts.
